structuralTasks.title: No green certified strategy
structuralTasks.detail: The monthly review could not find a strategy with a green deterministic certification.
structuralTasks.suggestedFix: Inspect failed certification rows, then adjust model policy artifacts or mine lower spend candidates without changing approval thresholds.

householdSignals.title: ACA bridge breach
householdSignals.headline: No selected yearly MAGI path attached
householdSignals.detail: The QA packet cannot confirm ACA bridge headroom without selected-policy yearly MAGI rows from certification.
householdSignals.recommendation: Run real certification so the AI review sees bridge-year MAGI, ACA premium, subsidy, and net-cost evidence.

clarity.whyItMatters: ACA bridge checks are only useful when the selected path exposes yearly MAGI, premium, subsidy, and net-cost rows.
clarity.whyItMayBeOk: A missing row can be acceptable only during a dry run or when certification has not produced healthcare evidence yet.
clarity.modelBugCheck: Treat this as a model evidence bug if real certification ran but selectedPathEvidence.yearlyRows is still empty.
clarity.decisionPrompt: Rerun real certification and confirm the 2027 bridge-year MAGI row is present before judging the ACA tradeoff.

householdSignals.title: Cash runway gap
householdSignals.headline: $53,571 short of 18-month target
householdSignals.detail: Direct cash is about 0.39 years of core spending and 8.7 months of essential-plus-fixed spending. The target is $103,571.
householdSignals.recommendation: Surface as a checkpoint item before Fidelity refresh: identify whether the gap should be filled from cash, taxable, or reduced payroll deferrals.

clarity.whyItMatters: Cash runway is an operational resilience gate, not a Monte Carlo solvency gate; it protects spending while transfers, payroll changes, or market sales are being handled.
clarity.whyItMayBeOk: This is a problem until a funding source, transfer plan, or explicit waiver is recorded.
clarity.modelBugCheck: Check for a bug if bank/cash balances are stale, cash held inside brokerage accounts is double-counted or excluded, or the essential-plus-fixed monthly target is not tied to current seed spending.
clarity.decisionPrompt: Choose whether to fill the cash bucket, document the source of backup liquidity, or intentionally waive the 18-month target.

householdSignals.title: Holding concentration
householdSignals.headline: FCNTX is 28% of portfolio
householdSignals.detail: Fidelity Contrafund is $257,816, or 28% of the total portfolio, and 100% of Authorized ROTH IRA.
householdSignals.recommendation: Show this as a concentration item: rebalance or reduce single-manager exposure inside tax-advantaged accounts where possible.

clarity.whyItMatters: A large single holding can dominate plan outcomes in ways the broad asset-class Monte Carlo model may not fully capture.
clarity.whyItMayBeOk: This is a problem until the household records a rebalance plan, staged rebalance, or explicit acceptance of concentration risk.
clarity.modelBugCheck: Check for a bug if duplicate holdings were merged incorrectly, account balances do not match holding totals, or fund tickers were imported into the wrong source account.
clarity.decisionPrompt: Decide whether to rebalance, stage the rebalance, or explicitly accept the single-holding exposure until the next review.

householdSignals.title: Legacy headroom
householdSignals.headline: Median legacy ratio unavailable
householdSignals.detail: The selected policy outcome does not include median ending wealth relative to the stated legacy target.
householdSignals.recommendation: Attach selected policy outcome percentiles before approving the spend recommendation.

clarity.whyItMatters: Legacy headroom can only be interpreted when the selected policy has ending-wealth percentiles.
clarity.whyItMayBeOk: Missing ending-wealth data is acceptable only in an incomplete diagnostic packet.
clarity.modelBugCheck: Treat as a model evidence bug if the selected policy has certification but no p50 ending wealth outcome.
clarity.decisionPrompt: Attach selected outcome percentiles and rerun before interpreting legacy headroom.

events.title: Salary until retirement transition
events.reviewerQuestion: Does wage income stop in the right year and flow through tax, spending, and contribution logic?

steps.detail: Wage income is modeled from salaryAnnual until salaryEndDate.
Salary end date controls the retirement transition year.
Salary is taxed as ordinary wage income and payroll contributions are modeled separately.
Wages fund annual cashflow first, with configured pre-retirement contributions directed into retirement accounts.

events.title: Social Security benefit stream
events.reviewerQuestion: Are claim ages, benefit amounts, survivor/spousal behavior, and tax treatment explicit enough for the household story?

steps.detail: Social Security benefit records are included for the household.
Claim ages are either explicit in the seed or supplied by the optimizer before projection.
Federal provisional-income Social Security taxation is modeled in the tax engine.
Benefits enter annual cashflow before portfolio withdrawals are calculated.

events.title: Cash-in event: inheritance
events.reviewerQuestion: Does this cash-in have an explicit source, timing, tax treatment, spend/use rule, destination account, and future investment policy?

steps.detail: Cash-in source is modeled as inheritance.
The event year is present, but certainty is not explicitly labeled.
Gross windfall amount is the modeled cash source.
Windfall tax treatment is explicitly modeled as cash_non_taxable.
Unspent cash-in is swept to a taxable shadow investment sleeve.
Same-year spending can use the received cash before any remainder is deployed.
Remainder is invested in the shadow sleeve using the aggregate current portfolio mix.
After deployment, the sleeve appreciates with market returns and remains available through normal taxable withdrawals.

events.title: Home sale proceeds
events.reviewerQuestion: Does this cash-in have an explicit source, timing, tax treatment, spend/use rule, destination account, and future investment policy?

steps.detail: Cash-in source is modeled as home_sale.
Timing and amount are modeled, but the event is marked estimated.
Home-sale cash available to the plan is explicit through liquidity or replacement-home assumptions.
Primary-home-sale tax treatment includes basis, exclusion, and selling-cost assumptions.
Unspent cash-in is swept to a taxable shadow investment sleeve.
Same-year spending can use the received cash before any remainder is deployed.
Remainder is invested in the shadow sleeve using the aggregate current portfolio mix.
After deployment, the sleeve appreciates with market returns and remains available through normal taxable withdrawals.

events.title: Household spending path
events.reviewerQuestion: Does the modeled monthly spend include the categories the household expects monthly to mean?

steps.detail: Core household spend is modeled from essential, optional, and taxes/insurance fields.
Travel is modeled as a separate early-retirement annual amount.

events.title: Portfolio withdrawal sourcing
events.reviewerQuestion: Are annual spending deficits sourced from the intended accounts and tax-aware withdrawal rule?

steps.detail: Withdrawal rule decides how cash, taxable, pretax, and Roth are used.
Pretax withdrawals, taxable withdrawals, Roth withdrawals, RMDs, and Roth conversions feed tax calculations separately.

events.title: Healthcare, tax, IRMAA, and LTC costs
events.reviewerQuestion: Are healthcare premiums, medical inflation, LTC assumptions, taxes, ACA, and IRMAA represented as cash outflows instead of narrative caveats?

steps.detail: Healthcare premiums and medical inflation are configured in rules.
Federal tax, ACA subsidy exposure, and IRMAA are modeled from yearly MAGI/tax inputs.
Long-term-care stress cost is explicit and can be offset by HSA strategy.

reviewerChecklist: Trace each major inflow from source to destination account.
Confirm whether each cash-in is spent, held as cash, invested, or earmarked.
Confirm tax treatment before accepting monthly-spend recommendations.
Flag internally consistent assumptions that do not match the household story.
Separate model facts, household decisions, and AI suggestions.

monthlyReviewIssueAnnotations.title: 2027 ACA subsidy loss is expected in the transition year
monthlyReviewIssueAnnotations.rationale: The 2027 bridge-year MAGI breach is a known transition-year tradeoff from final payroll/taxable income and the selected spend path, not a surprise failure. The plan should include the net ACA cost and can still be green if the certified path clears the gates.
monthlyReviewIssueAnnotations.decision: Accept the 2027 subsidy loss in the current review unless a MAGI-constrained alternative is explicitly requested to price the subsidy-preserving path.
evidence: Roth low-income bracket fill starts in 2028, after the final-paycheck transition year.
Contribution-limit assumptions are already labeled as planning_estimate_for_2027_final_paycheck_window.
Monthly review should still verify MAGI components and net ACA cost plumbing.
